ab: miwari u harem?
ch: M:你今天有空嘛?
en: Are you available today?

ab: kemuda ziyamaw?
ch: W:怎麼了?
en: What is the matter?

ab: maranger ku mukuwa muzangiya kaninu.
ch: M:我想去拜訪你。
en: I would like to visit you.

ab: ulra ku vangavangan harem, uniyan ku i ruma’. nu aidan lra mu.
ch: W:我今天有事不在家。改天吧。
en: I am out today. How about another day?

ab: nu kemazu mu, kaulra nu wari nu ’emanan? kay ku muzangiya kaninu nu piya kare’alri.
ch: M:那你明天有空嗎?我下午去找你。
en: Then, are you available tomorrow? I’ll visit you tomorrow afternoon.

ab: ai, ngangara ku i ruma’ kaninu.
ch: W:可以，我在家等你。
en: Sure, I will wait for you at home.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya, karuwa ku pasirep kana lrengaw? maranger ku venilin zanu vinavatiyan.
ch: M:請問我可以錄音嗎?我想做紀錄。
en: Would you mind if  I do the voice recording? I would like to keep a record.

ab: ai mu! kuwatreng za eman?
ch: W:好啊!沒有問題。
en: Sure! No problem.

ab: karuwa ta pasirep semasing?
ch: M:可以錄影嗎?
en: Would you mind if I do the video recording?

ab: amelri nu pana’uwan ku za trau, ’azi inepetr na puwalumayan zi, amanan!
ch: W:我不好看，倉庫也沒整理，不要啦。
en: No, I am plain and the warehouse hasn’t been tidied up yet.

ab: ’inava nu semasing ku nu kemazu.
ch: M:那我拍照就好。
en: Then I will just take photos.

ab: amanan semasing!
ch: W:拍照也不要!
en: No photographing either.

ab: lrayuwan za muwai u kikiumalr kaninku.
ch: M:謝謝你讓我訪問。
en: Thank you for accepting the interview.

ab: kakuda ziymaw, maranger u kiumalr za eman?
ch: W:沒問題，你想問什麼?
en: You are welcome. What would you like to know?

ab: kiumalra ku ziya, a eman nu kiyakarunan a lralrak u ziya?
ch: M:請問您年輕時是做什麼的?
en: What do you do when you are young?

ab: a kinsas ku a pakangaway, mawmu, meretra’ ku lra harem.
ch: W:我以前是警察，但我現在退休了。
en: I was a police officer but I am retired now.

ab: nu kemazu mu, ulra ziya nu kiyakarunan harem?
ch: M:那你現在有工作嗎?
en: Then, are you working at the moment?

ab: kiyakarun ku i takesiyan.
ch: W:我在學校上班。
en: I am working at school.

ab: kiumalra ku ziya, kaazu i zekalr nannu alrak?
ch: M:請問您的孩子也住在部落嗎?
en: Do your kids live in the tribe too?

ab: ’azi, kaazu i tokay nanku alrak.
ch: W:不，我的孩子在都市居住。
en: No, my kids are all living in the city.

ab: nu kemazu mu, kazu u sasa?
ch: M:那你自己一個人住嗎?
en: Then, do you live by yourself?

ab: temavur ku ziya za saya za suwan zi za saya za ’ayam, ninku niruma’enan nazu.
ch: W:我還有養一隻狗和一隻鳥，他們也是我的家人。
en: I also keep a dog and a bird. They are my family.

ab: temavur ku za saya za ngiyaw inku, nu milralrang ta za haramhaman mu, ulra ta kivangavangan.
ch: M:我也有養一隻貓，有動物陪伴不會無聊。
en: I have a cat too. I won’t get bored with the company of my pet.

ab: maranger ku kiumalr kaninu za tu palisiyan pa’alrupan.
ch: M:我想請問你關於打獵的禁忌。
en: I would like to ask you about the taboos of hunting.

ab: ’azi sazu ku kinalrazaman, mawmu, kunu tuvangaw zaku kinalrazaman ziya.
ch: W:我知道的不多，但我能回答的都會回答你。
en: I don’t know much about that, but I will tell you everything I know.

ab: ulra a eman palalisiyan kananmu kiyakarukarunan za pa’alrupan?
ch: M:你們打獵的武器有什麼禁忌嗎?
en: Do you have any taboos about hunting weapons?

ab: ’azi padikes kana vavayan zaniyam kiyakarukarunan za pa’alrupan.
ch: W:我們打獵的武器不可以讓女生碰到。
en: Our hunting weapons are untouchable to women.

ab: nu kemazu mu, karuwa mukasa mukuwa pa’alrupa na vavayan?
ch: M:那女人可以一起去打獵嗎?
en: Then, can women go hunting together?

ab: ’azi, ’azi karuwa pa’alrup na vavayan.
ch: W:不行，女人也不可以打獵。
en: No, women cannot go hunting either.
